title: Drones spawn at DRINZ = $1600, not at the lead's depth
claim: ROM: after PLNXCG copies the lead's 28-byte record into the drone slot, STPLNE overwrites the drone's Z MSB: `LDA I,DRINZ/100 / STA P.1ST+5` (RBARON.MAC:2369-2370), where DRINZ = $1600 = 5632 (RBARON.MAC:466) and `/100` is a HEX divide by $100, i.e. take the MSB $16. Object DB offset +5 is 'Z MSB' (glossary RBARON.MAC:273). The Z LSB (+4) is inherited from the lead's P.INDP = $1080 (RBARON.MAC:464), LSB $80 — so the drone's depth becomes $1680 = 5760 against the lead's $1080 = 4224: the drone starts 1536 units FARTHER out. The same store is repeated for drone #2 (`STA P.2ST+5`, RBARON.MAC:2384). Ours: `wave.push({ ...lead, kind: 'drone', x: lead.x + dx, y: lead.y + dy })` — the spread copies the lead's depth verbatim and only x/y are offset, so all three planes spawn at exactly the same Z.
reasoning: The drones are meant to be BEHIND the lead in depth as well as offset laterally — that is what makes the formation read as a formation and what makes the lead the natural first target. Spawning them coplanar with the lead flattens the wedge into a line abreast. The fix is one field in the drone spread; the Z offset (1536 world units in ROM Z, or the DRINZ − P.INDP difference re-expressed in our Z scale) is a constant, not a mechanism.

ours.verbatim: wave.push({ ...lead, kind: 'drone', x: lead.x + dx, y: lead.y + dy })

title: Mountain PLACEMENT is authored (PFOBIZ) — we generate an even stagger with all four at x = 0
claim: ROM: `PFOBIZ: .WORD 8200,6E0,3220,0D20` (RBARON.MAC:1305) — the four PF objects' INITIAL DEPTHS, hex: $8200 = 33280, $06E0 = 1760, $3220 = 12832, $0D20 = 3360 — followed by `.WORD -0C00,-400,400,0C00` (RBARON.MAC:1306) = their INITIAL X POSITIONS, hex: -$0C00, -$0400, +$0400, +$0C00 (GMINIT stores only the MSB: `LDA AY,PFOBIZ+9 ;X POSITION MSB`, RBARON.MAC:1268-1269). GMINIT then classifies each by its Z MSB — `CMP I,11 ;CHECK INITIAL POSITION / BCC 57$ / ORA I,80 ;ON HORIZON` (RBARON.MAC:1258-1261): $82 ≥ $11 → on horizon; $06 < $11 → already free; $32 ≥ $11 → on horizon; $0D < $11 → already free. So the arcade OPENS with 2 mountains on the horizon and 2 already fallen, spread across four lateral lanes. Ours: `initialMountains()` computes `gap = (4096 − 192) / 4 = 976` and lays the four slots at depths 4096, 3120, 2144, 1168 — an even arithmetic stagger — with `x: 0` for ALL FOUR.
reasoning: Two authored tables replaced by a formula. The depth stagger is arguably cosmetic; the X collapse is not — every mountain in our game occupies the same lateral lane (x = 0), so the four silhouettes stack directly on top of one another down the centre of the view instead of spanning the pass. The ROM's ±$400 / ±$C00 spread is what makes it a LANDSCAPE rather than a receding column. PFOBIZ is 8 words; transcribing it is a table, not a redesign.

title: The ROM has TWO mountain depth deltas ($180 on the horizon, $20 once fallen) — we have one (64)
claim: ROM PFOBMN runs two different closing rates. ON HORIZON (status D7 set → branch 45$): `LDA I,P.OBDZ&0FF ;STANDARD DELTA / ADC A,PRMDEL ;ADD PERCENT DELTA` (RBARON.MAC:3383-3384) with P.OBDZ = $180 = 384 (RBARON.MAC:444, ';PF OBJECT Y DELTA Z (WHILE ON HORIZON)'). ONCE FALLEN (free object): `LDA I,20 ;STANDARD DELTA / ADC A,PRPDEL ;ADD PERCENTAGING DELTA` (RBARON.MAC:3341-3342) with the literal $20 = 32, subtracted at RBARON.MAC:3345-3346. A mountain therefore closes 384/frame while distant and 32/frame while near — a 12:1 ratio, before the difficulty-percentage deltas PRMDEL/PRPDEL are added (PRMDEL = 4 × PRPDEL, RBARON.MAC:1871-1874). Ours: a single `const DEPTH_STEP = 64`, applied unconditionally in stepMountain, which our own comment concedes is 'a display-feel delta, not a ported ROM constant'.
reasoning: It IS a ported ROM constant — two of them. The near-field rate matters most for feel (a mountain sliding past the cockpit), and ours is 2× the ROM's $20 = 32; the far-field rate is 6× too slow. The comment in landscape.ts asserts 'ROM P.OBDZ/$180 counts raw ROM Z units; this port's Z is in scene world units' — but our Z IS in ROM units: SPAWN_DEPTH = HORZ = $1000 and MIN_DEPTH are both raw ROM constants, so the deltas can be ported directly with no rescale. The unit excuse does not survive contact with the module's own imports.

title: Mountains recycle to P.OBZI = $7F00 = 32512, not to HORZ = $1000 = 4096 — ours never spend time on the horizon
claim: ROM: when a free mountain reaches minimum depth, PFOBMN recycles it — `LDA AX,PFOBJ+6 ;ON HORIZON / ORA I,80` (RBARON.MAC:3358-3359), then `LDA I,PF.XZ/100 / STA AX,PFOBJ+3` (X depth ← $10, RBARON.MAC:3366-3367) and `LDA I,P.OBZI&0FF00/100 / STA AX,PFOBJ+5` (Z MSB ← $7F, RBARON.MAC:3368-3369), LSBs zeroed at :3361-3363. P.OBZI = $7F00 = 32512 (RBARON.MAC:443, ';PF OBJECT INITIAL Z'). Ours: `SPAWN_DEPTH = HORZ` = $1000 = 4096 — 7.94× too shallow. Arithmetic on the horizon dwell: the ROM's mountain closes from $7F00 to the fall threshold P.MAXZ = $1001 at P.OBDZ = $180/frame → (32512 − 4097) / 384 = 74.0 calc frames × 96 ms = 7.10 SECONDS as a distant silhouette on the horizon. Ours: spawns at exactly 4096 and the first `stepMountain` drops it to 4032 < 4096 → 0 frames. Our mountains are never on the horizon at all.
reasoning: P.OBZI ('PF OBJECT INITIAL Z') and HORZ ('HORIZON DEPTH') are different constants doing different jobs: HORZ/PF.XZ = $1000 is the X-projection depth used WHILE a mountain is a horizon silhouette; P.OBZI = $7F00 is where it is (re)born. Conflating them removes the entire approach phase — the 7-second build of a range rising out of the haze — and drops the player straight into the near field. This is why our landscape reads as 'popping in' rather than approaching. One constant.

title: The recycle threshold is a 16-bit compare against $01C0 = 448, not $C0 = 192
claim: ROM (RBARON.MAC:3349-3356): with the new depth LSB in Y and MSB in A, `CPY I,0C0 ;MINIMUM DEPTH / SBC I,1 / BCS 35$ ;NO`. That is the standard 6502 16-bit compare idiom — CPY against the LOW byte then SBC the HIGH byte — so the constant is ($01 << 8) | $C0 = $01C0 = 448, and the recycle fires when depth < 448. The same idiom appears twelve lines later against P.MAXZ: `CPY I,P.MAXZ&0FF / SBC I,P.MAXZ&0FF00/100 ;MAX Y DEPTH ?` (RBARON.MAC:3397-3398), where the assembler splits $1001 into its LSB and MSB — proving the pattern. Ours: `MIN_DEPTH = 0xc0 // 192`, taking only the CPY operand and dropping the SBC's high byte. The true value is 448; ours is 2.33× too close.
reasoning: Also a mis-citation: landscape.ts:38 cites 'RBARON.MAC:3349' for the $0C0, but 3349 is `TAY` — the CPY is at 3354. The error is small in effect (mountains linger 8 extra frames = 0.77 s at the ROM's $20 delta before recycling) but it is a value we got wrong from a half-read instruction pair, and the fix is one literal.

title: 'On horizon' is a latched STATUS BIT with hysteresis, not a depth comparison
claim: ROM: on-horizon is PFOBJ+6 bit D7 (object glossary RBARON.MAC:362-364, 'STATUS A X X X MTN#---, A ACTIVE'). PFOBMN dispatches on the LATCHED bit, not on depth: `10$: LDA AX,PFOBJ+6 ;STATUS / ... / BPL 15$ / JMP 45$ ;PF ON HORIZON` (RBARON.MAC:3294-3297). The bit is CLEARED once, when the object falls — `LDA AX,PFOBJ+6 ;ELSE START PF OBJECT 'FALL' / AND I,0F` (RBARON.MAC:3400-3401), reached only when depth < P.MAXZ = $1001 = 4097 (RBARON.MAC:445, tested at :3397-3399) — and SET once, at recycle (`ORA I,80`, RBARON.MAC:3359). It is a one-way latch per pass. Ours: `onHorizon(m) => m.depth >= HORZ`, recomputed from depth every call, with HORZ = $1000 = 4096 (the ROM's threshold is P.MAXZ = $1001 = HORZ + 1, and the sense is strictly-less-than, so our >= 4096 is off by one at the boundary too).
reasoning: The latch is not decoration: PFOBJ+6's LOW nibble simultaneously carries the MOUNTAIN DB OFFSET (glossary :364), and clearing D7 with `AND I,0F` is what PRESERVES that nibble while dropping the flag. Our predicate cannot express the latch because Mountain has no status field — the mountain's identity and its horizon state are the same byte in the ROM. Beyond structure, a depth-derived predicate is only equivalent to the latch if the recycle depth is right, and it is not (MI-016): today our mountains fail onHorizon() from frame 1 of every pass, so the horizon branch of the renderer is dead code.

title: Mountains never scroll laterally — the ROM subtracts PLYRDL every frame and wraps
claim: ROM: every free PF object has the player's lateral delta subtracted from its X each calc frame, then wrapped — `SEC / LDA AX,PFOBJ / SBC A,PLYRDL / STA AX,PFOBJ / TAY / LDA AX,PFOBJ+1 / SBC A,PLYRDL+1 / STA AX,PFOBJ+1 / JSR WRAPIT` (RBARON.MAC:3298-3306). At the moment of the fall the X is re-seeded from the horizon scroll position PFXSCR combined with the object's own mountain lane: `LDY PFXSCR / LDX PFXSCR+1 / JSR D.COMP` (RBARON.MAC:3403-3405) then `LDA I,-3 / ADC AX,PFOBJ+6 / ASL / ASL / CLC / ADC TEMP1 / AND I,1F` (RBARON.MAC:3411-3417) — the mountain index (PFOBJ+6 low nibble) × 4 sets its lane. Ours: `x` is written 0 at construction (`x: 0` in both spawnMountain and initialMountains) and stepMountain spreads `...m` without ever touching it. It is 0 forever, for every mountain, for the whole game.
reasoning: There is no lateral scroll and no lateral spread. Combined with MI-014 (all four seeded at x = 0), the entire landscape is a single column of silhouettes on the centre-line that grows toward the player and never slides. Banking or yawing the aircraft moves the camera but the mountains have nothing to move PAST — the ROM's WRAPIT band is what makes the pass feel endless. This needs an x-scroll term in stepMountain plus the PFXSCR/lane seeding, so it is a mechanism, not a constant.

ours.verbatim: return next <= MIN_DEPTH ? { ...m, depth: SPAWN_DEPTH } : { ...m, depth: next }

title: GREND — flying into a mountain kills you; we have no ground collision at all
claim: ROM: the ground-collision detector ORs the crashed mountain's index into GREND with D7|D6 — `LDA SAVX+1 ;MOUNTAIN OFFSET OF CRASH / ORA I,0C0 / STA GREND ;D6=GROUND COLLISION` (RBARON.MAC:4643-4645). The main loop reads it every frame, before any motion: `BIT GREND / BVS 20$ ;PLAYER RAN INTO GROUND` (RBARON.MAC:783-784), freezing PFMOTN, NWPLNE and PLMOTN; ENDLFE then takes a life (`DEC LIVES ;TAKE LIFE IN PLAY`, RBARON.MAC:1207) and the crash sequence redraws the mountain the player hit (`AND I,3F / TAX / LDA AX,PFOBJ+6 / AND I,0F / STA SAVY+1 ;MOUNTAIN #`, RBARON.MAC:1131-1135). The collision boxes are authored: PFOCOL, 24 PFCOL corners = 12 (min,max) pairs (RBGRND.MAC:824-847). Ours: landscape.ts exposes no collision surface, main.ts has no GREND, and topology.ts's transcribed PFOCOL table is imported by nobody — grep for PFOCOL outside topology.ts returns nothing.
reasoning: Red Baron's ground wave is a low-altitude run through a mountain pass; the mountains are the hazard. Without GREND the player can fly straight through a range, which removes the only reason the ground wave is tense. The PFOCOL data is already transcribed and sitting unused, so the ROM's half is done — what is missing is the box test, the GREND byte, and the main-loop freeze. That is a feature, not a constant.

title: scene.ts's far clip (20000) is below the ROM's farthest object (P.OBZI = 32512), and its HORZ comment repeats a radix error
claim: scene.ts:32-33 sets `const FAR = 20000` with the comment 'past the horizon distance (findings §7 HORZ = 1000, with headroom)'. HORZ is $1000 = 4096 (RBARON.MAC:451, `.RADIX 16`), not decimal 1000 — the comment reproduces the findings doc's un-radixed '1000' and reads as though 20000 were 20× the horizon when it is under 5×. More consequentially, the ROM's farthest playfield object sits at P.OBZI = $7F00 = 32512 (RBARON.MAC:443) and PFOBIZ seeds one mountain at $8200 = 33280 (RBARON.MAC:1305) — both BEYOND FAR = 20000. Fixing MI-016 (recycle to $7F00) without raising FAR would push every freshly recycled mountain outside the frustum and clip it away.
reasoning: This is latent today only because SPAWN_DEPTH is wrongly clamped to 4096, which keeps everything inside the far plane. It becomes a hard bug the instant MI-016 lands, so the two must move together. Raise FAR above 33280 (the PFOBIZ maximum) — 40000 gives headroom for the difficulty-percentage delta. The stale comment should also stop citing HORZ as decimal 1000.
